title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.2 Sponsor
NOAA / SUB / Petrobras

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.3 Project Purpose
Develop an Artificial Intelligence pipeline to automatically identify and filter benthic-relevant subsea data, supporting environmental baseline studies, monitoring activities, and regulatory compliance within Petrobras offshore operations.

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.4 Business Need
Subsea environmental datasets require extensive manual review. Automation is required to reduce workload, increase consistency, and accelerate environmental assessments.

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.5 Objectives

1.5.1 Primary Objective (MVP)
Develop a binary classification system to distinguish benthic-relevant from non-benthic data.


1.5.2 Secondary Objectives

Scalable multi-modal pipeline
AI-assisted annotation
Foundation for future detection, segmentation, and taxonomy

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.6 MVP Strategy
“Filter Noise, Preserve Confidence”

Precision-first approach
False positives acceptable
False negatives monitored as a critical environmental risk

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.7 Definition of Benthic-Relevant Data (MVP)
Includes: - Visible seabed substrate - Benthic organisms - Ecological structures (reefs, sediment patterns, bioturbation)
Excludes: - Open water column - Purely anthropogenic structures without biological presence - Unusable data (severe turbidity, blur, extreme lighting)

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.8 Success Criteria

1.8.1 Technical

Accuracy ≥ 60% (interpreted with class-level metrics)
Precision ≥ 75% for benthic-relevant class (primary KPI)



1.8.2 Operational

Dataset reduction ≥ 40–60%
Retention of ≥ 85–90% of true benthic data
Annotation time reduction ≥ 40%



1.8.3 Validation

Outputs validated by NOAA/SUB environmental specialists

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.6 High-Level Scope

1.6.1 In Scope

Still image classification (MVP)
Batch, offline processing
AI-assisted annotation
Human-in-the-loop validation



1.6.2 Out of Scope

Real-time deployment
Full taxonomic classification
Advanced multimodal fusion

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.9 Governance
Sponsor: NOAA / SUB
Project Manager: Assigned by SUB
Technical Authority: Data Science Lead
Scientific Authority: Marine/Environmental Lead

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.3 Purpose
Develop an Artificial Intelligence pipeline for automated identification and filtering of benthic-relevant subsea data to support environmental baseline studies under SUB responsibility.

title: 1  Project Charter
text:
1.7 High-Level Success Criteria

Precision ≥ 75% (benthic class)
Dataset reduction ≥ 40–60%
Retention ≥ 85–90% of true benthic data

title: 3  Scope Management Plan
text:
3.2 Definition of Benthic-Relevant Data
Includes visible seabed, benthic organisms, and ecological structures (reefs, sediment patterns, bioturbation).

title: 3  Scope Management Plan
text:
3.3 Explicit Exclusions

Open water column
Purely anthropogenic structures without biology
Unusable data (severe turbidity, blur, extreme lighting)

title: 3  Scope Management Plan
text:
3.4 MVP Strategy
Filter Noise, Preserve Confidence. Precision prioritized over recall; false negatives monitored as environmental risk.

title: 3  Scope Management Plan
text:
3.5 Deliverables

Binary classification model
Preprocessing pipeline
Annotation workflow
Validation reports
Versioned datasets

title: 3  Scope Management Plan
text:
3.6 Constraints

No labeled dataset
Class imbalance
Environmental variability
Offline-first requirement

title: 3  Scope Management Plan
text:
3.7 Assumptions
See Assumption Log.
